column1: # 01 YYYY   : Origin-Time of preferred location method - Year
# 02 MM     : Origin-Time of preferred location method - Month
# 03 DD     : Origin-Time of preferred location method - Day
# 04 HH     : Origin-Time of preferred location method - Hour
# 05 MI     : Origin-Time of preferred location method - Minute
# 06 SS     : Origin-Time of preferred location method - Second
# 07 Lat    : Preferred location method: latitude  (in deg)
# 08 Lon    : Preferred location method: longitude (in deg)
# 09 Dep    : Preferred location method: focal depth (below sea level
# 10 Mag    : Preferred location method: Magnitude as reported in bulletin
# 11 CAT-ID : ID of catalog used for preferred location:
#             -> BULLETIN   : Location is from SED (absolute) bulletin
#             -> ABSRELOC   : Location is from absolute-relocation catalog (the updated Diehl et. al. 2021 paper)

#             -> DDR-<ClID> : Location if from regional relative-relocation catalog. The ID of the regional catalog is indicated by <ClID> | NOTE: Relative errors of the regional catalogs should be ignored since biased by absolute shifts of clusters

#             -> DDC-<ClID> : Location if from local (cluster) relative-relocation catalog. The specific cluster is indicated by <ClID> | NOTE: Most reliable since most thorough QC. Absolute location can be shifted in respect to DDR solutions
# 12 ALQ    : Absolute location quality parameters: Location Quality A-D using a modified definition of Lee et al 2023 (GJI):
#             -> A : Gap < 160deg && Nobs >= 10 && RMS <= 0.5 s && MDIst <= 1.5*edep -> Well locatable in epicentre and depth
column2: absolute errors are likely realistic for epi and depth

column1: #             -> B : Gap < 160deg && Nobs >= 10 && RMS <= 0.5 s && MDIst >  1.5*edep -> Well locatable in epicentre
column2: absolute errors are likely realistic for epi
B and D                                            -> fairly well-locatable in epicentre and fairly-well to poorly locatable in depth

column1: #             -> D : Gap > 180deg || Nobs < 5   || RMS > 0.5 s                       -> Poorly locatable in epicentre and depth
# 13 AP     : Active plane e.g. from relative-relocations; 0: Unknown; 1: Plane-1; 2: Plane-2
# 14 S1     : Strike1 deg - Definition Aki & Richards
# 15 D1     : Dip1    deg - Definition Aki & Richards
# 16 R1     : Rake1   deg - Definition Aki & Richards
# 17 S2     : Strike2 deg - Definition Aki & Richards
# 18 D2     : Dip2    deg - Definition Aki & Richards
# 19 R2     : Rake2   deg - Definition Aki & Richards
# 24 FMQ    : Quality of focal mechanism (1-4) - 1: highest quality; not fully consistent over time and between Deichmann and Diehl. Only class 1-2 remain
# 25 FMT    : Type of focal mechanism as reported in original file (SS: Strike-Slip
# 26 ScR    : Scalar rake value indicating faulting regime following Mazzotti et al.: BSGF 2021 - -1: normal fault; 0: strike-slip; +1:thrust/reverse
# 27 ME     : Method either FM (from first-motion solution) or MT (from double-couple solution of moment tensor)
# 28 Mw     : Corresponding moment magnitude either from (in order of preferrance): MT
# 29 M0     : Corresponding seismic moment in Nm - from MT or converted from preferred Mw
# 30 EvID_KP: Event-ID following traditional SED definition (KP-system)
# 31 EvID_DD: Event-ID to be used for hypoDD relative relocations
# 32 CH-X   : CH-coordinates in m of preferred location method - 999000: outside of defined range
# 33 CH-Y   : CH-coordinates in m of preferred location method - 999000: outside of defined range
# 34 FMCAT-OTDa: Indicates origin-time date information originally reported in Deichmann+Diehl FM-catalog. Especially for events in 90ies and 80ies magnitudes
column2: depth and OT can deviate significantly

column1: # 35 FMCAT-OTTi: Indicates origin-time time information originally reported in Deichmann+Diehl FM-catalog. Especially for events in 90ies and 80ies magnitudes
column2: depth and OT can deviate significantly

column1: # 36 FMCAT-LAT: Indicates location    information originally reported in Deichmann+Diehl FM-catalog. Especially for events in 90ies and 80ies magnitudes
column2: depth and OT can deviate significantly

column1: # 37 FMCAT-LON: Indicates location    information originally reported in Deichmann+Diehl FM-catalog. Especially for events in 90ies and 80ies magnitudes
column2: depth and OT can deviate significantly

column1: # 38 FMCAT-DEP: Indicates location    information originally reported in Deichmann+Diehl FM-catalog. Especially for events in 90ies and 80ies magnitudes
column2: depth and OT can deviate significantly

column1: # 39 FMCAT-MAG: Indicates magnitude   information originally reported in Deichmann+Diehl FM-catalog. Especially for events in 90ies and 80ies magnitudes
column2: depth and OT can deviate significantly

column1: # 40 MiDis  : Distance to closest statiion in km of preferred absolute location (additional information for reliability of hypocenter)
# 41 Depi     : Control - Difference in epicenter (km) between preferred method and original solution in Deichmann+Diehl FM catalog
# 42 Ddep     : Control - Difference in f. depthr (km) between preferred method and original solution in Deichmann+Diehl FM catalog
# 43 Dmag     : Control - Difference in magnitude      between preferred method and original solution in Deichmann+Diehl FM catalog
# 44 Region : Region/Name provided in original FM-catalog
# 45 FMCAT-REF: Lists links with references provided in an external file of N. Deichmann (maintained by T. DIehl)
